headline: Long-running agent infrastructure dominated the week, with separated evaluation and persistent memory leading the rotation.
body: The week centered on architecture for production agents that survive across sessions: separated evaluation, subagents, and scheduled tasks all moved to the top of the rotation, anchored by a breakdown of three long-running patterns covering event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation [4]. Persistent memory was the recurring theme on the tooling side, with Hermes adding a unified memory store, skill library, and cron scheduling for locally deployed agents [0][1]. Enterprise plumbing advanced through Anthropic's organization-wide MCP connector authentication via identity providers [7], while Google showed on-device Gemma multimodal inference [3] and a multi-agent simulation SDK [6]. Observability and encoder-decoder both registered as breakouts alongside new entrants in distillation, robot policy, and ego-motion compensation.
